title: C++ error: cannot convert ‘VlQueue<short unsigned int>’ to ‘short unsigned int’ in assignment
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs)

```
module tb_fifo_2in;
  localparam int unsigned DATA_W = 16;
  logic [DATA_W-1:0] ref_q[$];
  int unsigned cyc      = 0;
  int unsigned n_cycles = 3000;
   initial begin
      logic [DATA_W-1:0] exp_q[$];
      for (cyc = 0; cyc < n_cycles; cyc++) begin
	 exp_q[$] = ref_q;
      end
      $finish;
   end
endmodule
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
In file included from Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined__ALL.cpp:8:
Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined___024root__DepSet_hcd35ad2a__0__Slow.cpp: In function ‘void Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined___024root___eval_initial__TOP(Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined___024root*)’:
Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined___024root__DepSet_hcd35ad2a__0__Slow.cpp:52:25: error: cannot convert ‘VlQueue<short unsigned int>’ to ‘short unsigned int’ in assignment
   52 |             = vlSelfRef.tb_fifo_2in__DOT__ref_q;
      |               ~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                         |
      |                         VlQueue<short unsigned int>
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

```
verilator --binary --sv -Wno-fatal sv-bugpoint-combined.sv

g++ -Os  -I.  -MMD -I/wss/tools/verilator/v5.041-gbe813e96d/share/verilator/include -I/wss/tools/verilator/v5.041-gbe813e96d/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVERILATOR=1 -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TIMING=0 -DVM_TRACE=0 -DVM_TRACE_FST=0 -DVM_TRACE_VCD=0 -DVM_TRACE_SAIF=0 -faligned-new -fcf-protection=none -Wno-bool-operation -Wno-int-in-bool-context -Wno-shadow -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-subobject-linkage -Wno-tautological-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-but-set-parameter -Wno-unused-but-set-variable -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable    -DVL_TIME_CONTEXT   -c -o verilated_threads.o /wss/tools/verilator/v5.041-gbe813e96d/share/verilator/include/verilated_threads.cpp
python3 /wss/tools/verilator/v5.041-gbe813e96d/share/verilator/bin/verilator_includer -DVL_INCLUDE_OPT=include Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined.cpp Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined___024root__DepSet_h42d71525__0.cpp Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined___024root__DepSet_hcd35ad2a__0.cpp Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined__main.cpp Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined___024root__Slow.cpp Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined___024root__DepSet_hcd35ad2a__0__Slow.cpp Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined__Syms.cpp > Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined__ALL.cpp

make: *** [/wss/tools/verilator/v5.041-gbe813e96d/share/verilator/include/verilated.mk:272: Vsv__02dbugpoint__02dcombined__ALL.o] Error 1
```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

```
Verilator 5.041 devel rev v5.040-103-gbe813e96d
```

What OS and distribution are you using?

```
Linux 5.14.0-570.37.1.el9_6.x86_64
```

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)
